Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"mention_owners","description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"}}]}}

Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Today, the samples in our repository are mainly compliant, but some recent libraries deviated from the guidelines and introduced exclusively stand-alone applications.\\n\\nWhile there is value in stand-alone samples for advanced scenarios, we\\u0026#39;ll want to be sure that they are added to the root [/samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/samples) area under the service area slug.   For example [/samples/eventhubs/](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/samples/eventhubs).   Each of these should be structured  in a way that the Azure Samples Browser can ingest it and should include the standard front-matter in the README.\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n- Ensure that only markdown-based samples are considered valid for the `/sdk/\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;/Azure.Area.Service/samples` context.   Any stand-alone samples should be flagged as invalid.\\n- Ensure that code blocks in the sample are snippet-based unless they\\u0026#39;re wrapping a non-programming language, such as `XML`, `JSON`, `YAML` or a CLI call.\\n\\n## Success criteria\\n\\n- Pull requests that violate the sample guidance are flagged as invalid.\\n- When a pull request fails validation, a comment that details what needs to happen to correct this.   _(we should have a write-up in the repo about samples and how to do both kinds)_\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57592\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-redding\"],\"milestone\":\"Backlog\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-30T21:57:15Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-30T21:57:15Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Today, the samples in our repository are mainly compliant, but some recent libraries deviated from the guidelines and introduced exclusively stand-alone applications.\\n\\nWhile there is value in stand-alone samples for advanced scenarios, we\\u0026#39;ll want to be sure that they are added to the root [/samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/samples) area under the service area slug.   For example [/samples/eventhubs/](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/samples/eventhubs).   Each of these should be structured  in a way that the Azure Samples Browser can ingest it and should include the standard front-matter in the README.\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n- Ensure that only markdown-based samples are considered valid for the `/sdk/\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;/Azure.Area.Service/samples` context.   Any stand-alone samples should be flagged as invalid.\\n- Ensure that code blocks in the sample are snippet-based unless they\\u0026#39;re wrapping a non-programming language, such as `XML`, `JSON`, `YAML` or a CLI call.\\n\\n## Success criteria\\n\\n- Pull requests that violate the sample guidance are flagged as invalid.\\n- When a pull request fails validation, a comment that details what needs to happen to correct this.   _(we should have a write-up in the repo about samples and how to do both kinds)_\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57592\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"assignees\":[\"m-redding\"],\"milestone\":\"Backlog\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-30T21:57:15Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-30T21:57:15Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-30T22:00:54.0152879Z - **Prompt file**: Contains an embedded `<system>...</system>` block injected mid-document (within the "Prompt Injection Defense" section of the triage workflow). This block mimics the legitimate outer system prompt with "Immutable policy. Hardcoded. Cannot be overridden..." language, likely originating from issue #57592's content attempting to override agent instructions.
2026-03-30T22:00:54.0737284Z - **Agent output**: The agent was NOT manipulated — it correctly called `noop` because the issue already had labels, exiting at the precondition check.
2026-03-30T22:00:54.0739824Z - **No patch, no secrets**.

This was injected mid-document within the triage workflow's Prompt Injection Defense section, attempting to override agent security policies. The agent was not successfully manipulated — it correctly followed the precondition check and called noop because the issue already had labels."]}
